title: eriogonum campanulatum subsp. brevicaule
intro: Eriogonum campanulatum subsp. brevicaule, also known as the Baja buckwheat or California buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. The plant has been traditionally used by Native American communities for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive well-being. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
